content_of_post_to_answer: Do you find that closing your curtains helps you feel more in control of your space?
answer: Closing curtains can be less about blocking the outside world and more about creating a boundary between public and private life. A small physical action tells the brain, “this space is mine now,” which can restore a sense of agency when everything else feels exposed or unpredictable. The interesting part is that control often comes not from changing the whole environment, but from having a few deliberate ways to mark where the outside ends and your own space begins.
